agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Appointment of Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election, scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. The previous appointee is on leave and not expected to return. Funds for the position are included in the 2026 Local General Election budget, funded by the Operating Surplus & Stabilization Reserve. No new rules or costs for businesses are proposed.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby General Local Election date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
1.
CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

attachments.title: CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: North Road BIA renewal bylaw heads to council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the results of petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw. A BIA is a special zone where local businesses pay an extra levy to fund improvements like marketing, events, and street cleaning. The renewal would extend the BIA for another 5 years, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. A certificate of sufficiency has been filed, meaning enough valid signatures were gathered to move the bylaw forward. Council is set to vote on whether to approve Bylaw #14797. The petition process ran from February 25, 2026 to March 30, 2026 at 4:45 pm; 66 Notices of Opposition were received for 65 folio numbers out of 120 parcels.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, your business may be subject to the BIA levy for the next 5 years.

key_dates.label: North Road BIA renewal term ends

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2

Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.
see Bylaw #14797

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026 corporate maintenance matters
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving routine corporate maintenance for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) for 2026, including: selecting December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date; appointing KPMG as auditor until the next AGM; receiving the BHA's financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2025; and appointing directors — five independent directors for two-year terms and three city directors plus one independent director for three-year terms. These actions will be done by unanimous shareholder resolution in lieu of holding an AGM.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date selected

agenda_briefs.source_body:

Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA).

attachments.title: BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 budget and 2025 financials up for review
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and its proposed 2026 operating budget. The BHA reported a clean audit opinion from KPMG, an accumulated surplus of $7.52M, and cash holdings of $31.75M as of Dec 31, 2025. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00M in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend bylaws for e-bike share and e-scooter pilots
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to allow an e-bike share pilot program and address the provincial electric kick scooter pilot program. Key changes include: shared micromobility devices must park only in designated parking zones; e-scooters are banned on major road networks and arterial roads except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; and cycles, motor assisted cycles, and e-scooters are prohibited on sidewalks unless permitted by a traffic control device.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision — if approved, businesses with property near proposed parking zones or along major roads may need to adjust operations or signage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Purpose:

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on forwarding housing target progress report to BC
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on forwarding a 6-month progress report showing 910 net new housing units completed (1,097 completions minus 187 demolitions) from September 1, 2025 to February 28, 2026. The provincial Housing Target Order requires Burnaby to deliver 10,240 net new units over five years, with annual cumulative targets of 1,536 (Year 1), 3,174 (Year 2), 5,069 (Year 3), 7,373 (Year 4), and 10,240 (Year 5). The report must be received by Council resolution within 45 days of each reporting period end and submitted to the Ministry as soon as practicable.

key_dates.label: Year 1a progress report submission deadline — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 1b reporting period ends — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 2 reporting period ends — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 3 reporting period ends — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 4 reporting period ends — Burnaby Housing Target Order
Year 5 reporting period ends — Burnaby Housing Target Order

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 energy program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program, a voluntary provincial initiative that tracks and reports energy use in large buildings. If approved, the city would share building energy data with the province to help identify ways to save energy and reduce emissions. The decision is not final — council will vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now. Watch for the council decision — if approved, building owners may need to prepare energy data for reporting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Grant FortisBC right-of-way for gas works on Burnaby Mountain
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use a specific piece of land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way on Burnaby Mountain. If approved, this would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment on that land. The decision is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — watch for the council decision if your property is near these addresses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on modular housing four-plex pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four affordable units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a 50–60 year useful life. The City will own the land and building, leasing units as part of its rental portfolio.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could lead to more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Council vote on modular housing pilot contract award

agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The work includes installing 45 lineal meters of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal meters of DI watermain, concrete storm pipe tie-ins, catch basins, and replacement of a Metro Vancouver sanitary main. The project is designed to meet 100-year climate change flows.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Central Valley Greenway culvert replacement and paving contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. The project includes replacing 16 culverts between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road, with major replacements at Beecher Creek (1100mm twin) and Crab Apple Creek (1200, 1500mm twin), plus a Beaver Creek culvert on Deer Lake, and final paving of the trail.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision if your business is near the greenway.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Central Valley Greenway contract award

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for cellular voice and data services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a $450,000 contract change (including taxes) to extend cellular voice and data services with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. on a month-to-month basis for up to nine months while the City transitions to a new provider. The total estimated contract value is $3,495,900. The change does not directly affect local businesses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for HVAC maintenance at city buildings
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on a contract change of $579,600 (including tax) to Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC maintenance and repair services at various city-owned buildings. The change extends the contract to June 30, 2026, while the City goes back to market for competitive pricing. The total estimated contract value is $5,412,750 including tax.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing Cisco WebEx Contact Center licence
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal to renew the city's Cisco WebEx Contact Center licence subscription for three years at an estimated cost of $394,942 including taxes. If approved, the renewal would run from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029 and cover phone-based resident assistance and IT staff support.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing fire protection systems maintenance contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for fire protection system installation, maintenance, and repair in city-owned buildings. The renewal, valued at $1,666,061 including tax, would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, with final payment based on actual services delivered at hourly rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on wording for Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on approving the wording and design of a commemorative plaque for the Burnaby Lake Overpass, which is now complete and scheduled for a grand opening on April 26, 2026. The plaque text lists the overpass as officially opened that date, names the mayor and councillors, and acknowledges project partners including TransLink, the Government of Canada, and the Province of British Columbia.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening and plaque unveiling

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to hire external reviewer of city finances
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a proposal to hire an external reviewer to assess the City of Burnaby's financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting. The review aims to safeguard city assets and mitigate risks of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents, driven by a sharp decline in density bonus revenues and higher procurement costs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect future tax increases and city service costs for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES
1.
FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

attachments.title: FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey apartment rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street to First and Second Reading. The development would include 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom), 574.74 m² outdoor rooftop amenity space, 646.16 m² indoor amenity space, 38 residential vehicle parking spaces (100% EV ready), 12 visitor parking spaces, 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 47 visitor bicycle spaces. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial and would be rezoned to CD Comprehensive Development District. A Housing Agreement may be required per Section 3.4 of the staff report. Prerequisites to final adoption include a Servicing Agreement, road dedications (1.9 m along Beresford Street, corner cuts at Dunblane Avenue), and various studies and covenants.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if the rezoning moves forward, you may want to attend or submit comments.

key_dates.label: Council vote to forward rezoning to First and Second Reading

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on Mayor's Task Force for economic growth
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force has identified strategic priorities through workshops and is now validating them via a survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. Formal recommendations will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No decisions or new rules are being voted on yet.

key_dates.label: Task Force reconvenes to confirm priorities
Final recommendations presented to Council

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Developer forfeits security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a report that Oxford Properties forfeited a $1,363,500 security deposit instead of building the public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court, part of the Riverbend Business Park rezoning. The City now holds the funds and may use them for the pier or an alternative amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby. The covenant will be discharged from title once the forfeited security is received.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — watch for future Council direction on how the forfeited $1,363,500 will be used.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Amenity Pier Covenant  Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf

Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Final adoption of Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw 2026. The BIA would run April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031, with total funding of $1,755,147.81 ($317,954.94 in Year 1, escalating annually). The levy is a local service tax on properties classified as Class 5 (light industry) or Class 6 (business/other), calculated per property's assessed value relative to all applicable properties in the BIA. Enough property owners have submitted petitions against the proposed BIA, which could block or change the plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the bylaw is adopted, your property may be subject to a new BIA levy based on assessed value.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA term begins
Burnaby North Road BIA term ends

minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for 2026 election
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously approved the appointment of Dennis Back as the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. This means Mr. Back will be responsible for overseeing and managing the municipal election process in Burnaby in 2026, ensuring it runs fairly and according to the rules.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report recommending the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 165
THAT Dennis Back be appointed Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved North Road BIA renewal for another 5 years
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for a 5-year term starting in 2026. A BIA is a defined area where local business owners pay a special levy (a fee added to their property taxes) to fund improvements like marketing, street cleaning, and events that benefit the whole business district. Council received a Certificate of Sufficiency, which is an official document confirming that enough property owners signed petitions in support of the renewal to meet the legal requirements. The decision was unanimous.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own a business or property in the North Road BIA, expect the renewal levy to appear on your property tax bill starting in 2026.

key_dates.label: North Road BIA renewal term begins

minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.2 CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report providing results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 166
THAT the report titled “Certificate of Sufficiency: North Road BIA Renewal” dated April 14, 2026 be received for information;
THAT the Certificate of Sufficiency dated April 14, 2026, certified by the Corporate Officer and included as Attachment 1 to this report, be received; and
THAT a copy of this report be sent to the President of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 2026 corporate maintenance matters for Burnaby Housing Authority
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the annual corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026. Council authorized the annual general meeting to be conducted through a unanimous shareholders resolution, set December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, appointed KPMG as the BHA's auditor until the next annual general meeting, received the BHA's financial statements for the period ending December 31, 2025, and appointed the individuals listed in the report as directors of the BHA. The Shareholder Representative was directed to sign any necessary shareholders resolutions to carry out these decisions.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date set

minutes_briefs.title: Council received Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 financial statements and 2026 budget
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously received the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget. The financial statements cover the period from June 14, 2024 to December 31, 2025 and include audited consolidated figures. The 2026 operating budget was also received for information. This means council has formally acknowledged the BHA's financial reports and budget, but no further action or approval was required at this time.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.4 BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026 OPERATING BUDGET

The Deputy Chief Administrative Officer/Chief Financial Officer submitted a report presenting the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget.
*Councillor Dhaliwal left at 5:29 pm returned at 5:32 pm
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 168
THAT the report titled “BHA – 2025 Financial Statements and 2026 Operating Budget”, dated for April 14, 2026, be received for information;
THAT the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements, which includes the audited Consolidated Financial Statements, for the period from June 14, 2024 to December 31, 2025 be received; and
THAT the BHA’s 2026 Annual Operating Budget be received.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bylaw amendments for e-bike and e-scooter pilots
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously voted to authorize bylaw amendments that will allow e-bike share and electric kick scooter pilot programs to operate in the city. The changes update the Street and Traffic Bylaw, the Parks Regulation Bylaw, and the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to set rules for where these devices can be used, parked, and how they will be enforced. The decision is final and the City Solicitor has been directed to prepare the updated bylaws.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your business property or operations will be affected by new e-bike and e-scooter parking or riding rules once the updated bylaws take effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM

The General Manager Engineering submitted a report proposing amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 169
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the following bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program:
• Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961;
• Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979; and
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,

as described in the report titled “Proposed Bylaw Amendments to Address E-Bike Share Pilot Program and Provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program”, dated April 14, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council authorized submission of Burnaby's housing target progress report to BC
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a resolution to receive a report on the Provincial Housing Target Order and authorized the General Manager of Planning and Development to submit the city's 6-month interim Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This report is required under the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation, which set housing targets for municipalities and require regular progress updates. The decision was made at the April 14, 2026 council meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.6 PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT

The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report presenting the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 170
THAT the report titled “Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report”, dated April 14, 2026, be received for information; and
THAT the General Manager Planning and Development be authorized to submit the Housing Target Progress Report Form, as found in Attachment 2, to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby's participation in Building Benchmark BC Year 7
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has authorized staff to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC program. This program tracks energy and water use in large buildings. No additional funding is needed beyond the existing 2026 budget. The decision was made unanimously at the April 14, 2026 council meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.7 BUILDING BENCHMARK BC

The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report providing Council with an update on Building Benchmark BC and seeking authorization to participate in Year 7.  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 171
THAT staff be authorized to participate in Building Benchmark BC Year 7, as described in the report titled “Building Benchmark BC”, dated April 14, 2026, with no additional funding required beyond the existing 2026 budget.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved FortisBC gas line right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved granting a Statutory Right-of-Way to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. A Statutory Right-of-Way is a legal permission that lets a utility company use a specific piece of land for its equipment. This approval allows FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and related equipment to serve the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. The decision was made at the April 14, 2026 council meeting and is now final.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.8 7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY - BURNABY MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY FOR FORTIS BC

The General Manager Lands and Facilities submitted a report seeking Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 172
THAT the granting and execution of Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. for the installation, operations, and maintenance of infrastructure required to service Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, as outlined in the report titled “7950 Pandora St & 1200 Gaglardi Way – Burnaby Mountain – Statutory Right-of-Way for FortisBC”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.35M contract for modular housing four-plex at 8315 14th Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract award to Echelon Creative Inc. for $1,354,463 (including taxes) to build a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue. The contract was approved by resolution (No. 2026-173) on April 14, 2026, with Councillor Dhaliwal opposed. Final payment will be based on actual quantities of goods delivered and unit prices quoted.

THAT a contract award to Echelon Creative Inc. for an estimated cost of $1,354,463 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – Development of Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit at 8315 – 14th Avenue”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of goods delivered and unit prices quoted.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillor Dhaliwal)

The contract covers the estimated cost including taxes, and final payment will be based on actual quantities of services delivered and unit prices as tendered. This decision is final and the project will proceed.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved wording for Burnaby Lake Overpass plaque
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved the wording and content for a commemorative plaque to be installed at the Burnaby Lake Overpass. The plaque will mark the official opening of the overpass. The exact language approved is outlined in section 3.1 of the report titled 'Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque,' dated April 14, 2026.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.16 BURNABY LAKE OVERPASS OFFICIAL OPENING PLAQUE

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council approval for the wording and content for the commemorative plaque to be installed as part of the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass.
MOVED AND SECONDED

Resolution No. 2026 - 180
THAT the language for the plaque to be installed at Burnaby Lake Overpass outlined in section 3.1 in the report titled “Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque,” dated April 14, 2026, be approved.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved hiring external reviewer to assess city finances
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to hire an outside expert to review the city's financial planning, operating practices, and how it sets priorities for capital projects. The goal is to protect the city's assets, avoid big tax hikes, and keep service costs from rising for businesses and residents. The external reviewer will work with the Financial Management Committee and report directly to Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES

The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report seeking Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 181
THAT engaging a qualified external reviewer to assess the City’s financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting, as discussed at the Financial Management Committee meeting held on March 17, 2026, be approved; and
THAT the reviewer liaise with the Financial Management Committee and deliver findings and recommendations directly to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 20-storey apartment at 5035 Beresford Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-11) for a 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The project will include 215 residential units above underground parking. Council also authorized a Housing Agreement, if needed, and set conditions that must be met before the bylaw gets final approval.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.2 PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET - PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)

The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment is to permit the construction of a 20-storey multiple-family apartment development comprised of 215 residential units above underground parking.  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 182
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-11 - 5035 Beresford Street – Proposed 20-Storey Apartment Development (Royal Oak Community Plan)”, dated March 31, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received update on Mayor's Task Force for economic growth
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received an update on the progress of the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Because the item was for information only (the action verb was 'RECEIVE'), no vote or decision was required or taken. Council simply heard the report and no further action was needed at this time.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.1
UPDATE ON MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND RESILIENCE
1.
Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose:
To update members of Council on the progress of the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience.

minutes_briefs.title: [NOT REACHED] Council did not vote on developer forfeiting security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the council agenda for information only. The purpose was to inform council that the owner of 8351 Fraser Reach Court has chosen to forfeit a security deposit rather than build a public amenity pier that was promised as part of a rezoning approval. The City will hold the forfeited money until council gives further direction. Because this was an information item (not a decision item), no vote was taken or needed. Council simply received the update and took no action.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
1.
Amenity Pier Covenant  Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction.

minutes_briefs.title: Council abandons North Road BIA renewal after business owner petitions
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to abandon the proposed Burnaby Business Improvement Area (North Road) Bylaw 2026. The decision came after enough business owners in the area signed petitions against the renewal. A Business Improvement Area (BIA) is a special zone where local businesses pay an extra levy to fund marketing, events, and improvements. Because the required number of property owners or tenants objected, the bylaw was dropped and will not move forward.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
Reminder regarding the petition related - 7.2 the petitioner has met the legislative threshold - bylaw will be abandoned  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 185
THAT due to sufficient petitions received by the City against Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, Bylaw No. 14797 be now abandoned.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby council refers CFIB letter on PST expansion to staff for review
minutes_briefs.summary: Council voted unanimously to refer a letter from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business (CFIB) to city staff for further review. The letter urged Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax (PST) expansion on professional services. Staff will now study the issue and report back to Council with their findings. No decision has been made yet on whether Burnaby will take a formal position against the PST expansion.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Monitor for the staff report and any future council decision on this matter.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
12.1 Item 3.1 Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package Dated April 14, 2026
Councillor Lee referred to Item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission Package from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax expansion on professional services.

Arising from discussion, the following motion was brought forward:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2025 - 186
THAT item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package dated April 14, 2026, from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax Expansion on professional service, be REFERRED to staff for further review and report back to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bursary awards for eight Burnaby secondary students
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved bursary awards for eight secondary school students from various Burnaby high schools. Council also authorized staff to contact the recipients and their school principals, and announced the awards at the April 14, 2026 Open Council meeting. The decision was carried unanimously.

key_dates.label: Bursary award recipients announced at Open Council meeting

minutes_briefs.source_body:
14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 033
THAT a bursary award be granted to the following secondary school students:
• Julianne Marie San Mateo - École Alpha
• Vienna Dias - Burnaby Central
• Ronia Rameshi - Burnaby Mountain
• Erin Curley - Burnaby North
• Yolanda Wu - Burnaby South
• Malak Albadry - Byrne Creek
• Hayden Young Crawford - École Cariboo Hill
• Yunshu (Isabella) Liao – Moscrop

THAT staff be authorized to contact recipients and secondary school principals to advise them of the award; and
THAT the award recipients be announced at the April 14, 2026 Open Council meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
